title: rosa agrestis subvar. longipes
intro: Rosa agrestis subvar. longipes, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa agrestis plant, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible hips. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its rich content of vitamins A and C, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Rosa agrestis subvar. longipes include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Rosa agrestis subvar. longipes include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make jams, jellies, and other culinary products, as well as for medicinal purposes.
